seoTitle: How to Build a Wedding RSVP Website in Wix
metaDescription: Learn how to build a wedding website with digital RSVP tracking in Wix using the Events app and custom guest forms.
intro: Wix makes it possible to build a personalized wedding website complete with a digital RSVP system so you can track guest responses without a stack of paper cards. This guide covers picking a wedding template, adding your event details, and using the Wix Events app to collect RSVPs, meal preferences, and guest counts.

steps.heading: Choose a wedding template
steps.body: Browse the Wix template library and select a design framework suited to a wedding website.

steps.heading: Customize your banner and typography
steps.body: Update your landing banner and font styles to match your event's visual tone.

steps.heading: Install the Wix Events app
steps.body: Add the official Events and Tickets module from the App Market to power your registration system.

steps.heading: Add your event details
steps.body: Fill in your ceremony timeline, venue location, and any registry links guests will need.

steps.heading: Build your RSVP form
steps.body: Customize the registration form to collect meal choices, guest counts, and other details you need.

steps.heading: Set privacy and test
steps.body: Optionally restrict access with a password or invitation matching, then preview the RSVP flow before publishing.

faq.question: Can I collect meal preferences through the RSVP form?
faq.answer: Yes, you can customize the RSVP registration form to include questions about meal choices and other guest details.

faq.question: Can I keep my wedding site private?
faq.answer: Yes, Wix lets you restrict access using page passwords or invitation matching so only your intended guests can view it.

faq.question: How do I see who has RSVP'd?
faq.answer: Your back-end dashboard panel shows live submission updates and lets you download guest lists and catering sheets.

faq.question: Do I need a separate app for RSVP tracking?
faq.answer: The Wix Events app handles registration and RSVP tracking natively, so no separate third-party tool is required.
